sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 136 of 2023 ITRA 137 of 2023 ITRA 138 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objections No.24 & 25. 2. For orders on CMA No.161/2023. 3. For hearing of main case. 20.04.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, advocate for the applicant. Matter is pending since 2023 without any progress. Diary demonstrates that in preceding three years applicant has made no effort ot have the matter listed or heard. On 06.04.2026 following order was passed: “06.04.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, advocate for the applicant. Learned counsel seeks time to reformulate questions of law. At his request to come up on 20.04.2026. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in connected matters.” Today learned counsel remains unable to articulate any question of law arising here from. It appears that applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy hereof in the connected files. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
